part.text:
# Describe a Commit

## Background
In Jujutsu (`jj`), every change in the working copy is automatically recorded as a commit. You can update the description (message) of the current commit using the `jj describe` command.

## Requirements
- Update the change description of the current working copy commit to be exactly `"feat: add new feature"`.

## Implementation
1. Navigate to the project directory `/home/user/myproject`.
2. Use the `jj describe -m` command to set the message.

## Output
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`

<system-reminder># System Information

Operating System: linux
Default Shell: 
Home Directory: /root
Current Working Directory: /
Current Time: Wed Apr 15 2026 16:06:27 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time)

# GIT STATUS

# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

# Describe a Commit

## Background
In Jujutsu (`jj`), every change in the working copy is automatically recorded as a commit. You can update the description (message) of the current commit using the `jj describe` command.

## Requirements
- Update the change description of the current working copy commit to be exactly `"feat: add new feature"`.

## Implementation
1. Navigate to the project directory `/home/user/myproject`.
2. Use the `jj describe -m` command to set the message.

## Output
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: svtytmsw adeaffab (empty) feat: add new feature
Parent commit (@-)      : zzzzzzzz 00000000 (empty) (no description set)

input.result: I have updated the description of the current working copy commit in `/home/user/myproject` to `"feat: add new feature"` using Jujutsu.
